topic: Eclipse magnetic ensemble (39 eclipses) — Kim & Chang (2018)
change_summary: Change detection flagged detail_evidence growth 2323->3009 chars (+30%) and detail_verdict 1958->1995 (+2%) since c6 fingerprint. Decider self-applied four of five c6 hole recommendations: P4 count-inflation harmonized to WIN-025 phrasing (hole 1), P3 gained the 'ionospheric parameters measured independently... dome FSF circular' clause (hole 2, the defense_survives=3 neutralizer), P2 gained the 'not the Std Model Explains verdict above' inoculation (hole 3 option a), detail_verdict P4 now anchors to #timestamp-error (hole 4). Yamazaki DOI remains unverified (hole 5 open). Net: successful patch cycle; two moderate residual phrasing issues and some citation-pinning minors to probe.
our_argument_summary: Verdict: Std Model Explains. Three pillars hold: (1) Chapman 1933 Sq ionospheric mechanism predicts the ensemble signal from first principles — E-region conductivity drop during shadow passage produces the observed Y+/X-/Z-/F- perturbation pattern; (2) the dome provides only an empirically-fitted FSF scaling formula (0.642-2.075 = 3.2x spread across nine European stations at similar disc radii), with no derivation from disc_radius, firmament_height, or aetheric parameters; (3) count inflation — WIN-010 (BOU 2017 single-station), WIN-025 (2024 9-station), and WIN-068 (39-eclipse 100-station 1991-2016 ensemble) are three WINs invoking one Sq mechanism at three aggregation levels. New in c6 patch: P3 distinguishes standard-Sq calibration (independently-measured ionospheric parameters) from dome FSF calibration (directly fitted to the eclipse signal); P2 refraction-cascade reference carries explicit inoculation stating it does not bear on the verdict. Tier 1 Steelman Evidence Ladder admission preserved. Sibling WINs now share consistent count-inflation phrasing.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: No automation for the 39-eclipse ensemble. PROS-003 tracks BOU 2017 and 2024 9-station (→ WIN-010, WIN-025). WIN-068 is a retrospective literature citation.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: N/A — no monitor.py automation. Kim & Chang ensemble signal asserted qualitatively.
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: None on WIN-068. WIN-010 has Kp<2 precondition escape on the BOU event.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: monitor.py contains no domain for the Kim & Chang 39-eclipse ensemble. PROS-003 tracks BOU 2017 (→WIN-010) and 2024 9-station (→WIN-025). Ensemble is a retrospective literature citation. Tag unchanged from c6.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Chapman 1933 Sq-current framework (refined by Yamazaki & Maute 2021; Takeda & Araki 1984) predicts ensemble component signs, latitude peak 30-50°, and ~180-min timescale from measured/climatological ionospheric parameters, with no dome parameters required. Dome relabels mechanism as aetheric cavity shading — same predicted pattern, new vocabulary. Tag unchanged.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Kim & Chang 2018 ASR (published 2018); dome incorporated in V51.0 (early 2026). Eight-year gap confirmed in detail_verdict P4. No venue-independent pre-registration of expected values. Tag unchanged.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: No derivation from H(r)=8537*exp(-r/8619), disc_radius, or aetheric parameters produces Kim & Chang's specific component/latitude/timescale pattern. FSF formula is empirically fitted to station data. Arithmetic check (see quantitative_verification) shows FSF 3.2x spread would require ~10,000 km disc-radius variation — implausible for European stations spanning ~4,200 km. Tag unchanged.

holes_found.description: The c6 hole-2 patch ('ionospheric parameters Sq models use are measured independently by ionosondes and satellite instruments — not derived from the eclipse signal being predicted') partially neutralizes the 'both sides calibrate' defense but overstates the independence. Modern Sq models (Yamazaki & Maute 2021; Richmond & Maute 2014; TIE-GCM) do NOT in fact use eclipse-specific ionosonde measurements during each event — they use climatological electron density profiles (IRI), empirical conductivity tensors tuned to global TEC and magnetometer data, and photochemical models that calculate eclipse-time depletion theoretically. The ionospheric inputs are 'independent of the eclipse signal' in the decisive sense of not being fitted to the eclipse magnetic output — but calling them 'measured independently by ionosondes and satellite instruments' during the eclipse is imprecise and opens a rebuttal. A sharp defender will cite Yamazaki & Maute 2021 and analogous Sq-modeling literature to show Sq predictions rely on climatological/modeled inputs, then argue the 'independence' distinction is rhetorical. The correct framing is: standard Sq models predict the eclipse signal from ionospheric inputs obtained in *other contexts*, while dome FSF is fitted to the eclipse signal it purports to predict — the circularity is in the output-to-input loop, not in the provenance of every input parameter.
holes_found.recommendation: Tighten P3 phrasing to: '...the ionospheric parameters Sq models use (E-region electron density, conductivity tensors) are obtained from independent sources — ionosonde networks, GPS/TEC, and climatological averages — none of which are fitted to the eclipse magnetic signal being predicted. The dome FSF, by contrast, is calibrated directly against the eclipse observations it claims to predict, closing a circular loop that standard Sq models avoid.' Preserves the circularity contrast without overstating eclipse-time ionosonde coverage.

holes_found.description: P4 count-inflation phrasing ('inflating the score count without adding independent evidence') is rhetorically strong but arguably inaccurate on 'independent evidence.' Different events — BOU 2017 (WIN-010), 2024 American eclipse (WIN-025), and the 1991–2016 INTERMAGNET ensemble (WIN-068) — ARE independent observations of the Sq mechanism. The dome defender's rebuttal writes itself: 'We do not triple-count one observation; we cite three distinct data sources at single/regional/global aggregation levels. That is methodological strengthening, not count inflation.' Our critique should be sharper: the three WINs count as three separate confirmed *dome predictions* in the 67-WIN total, but they collectively support only one *non-discriminating* mechanism. The correct lever is predictive novelty, not observational independence.
holes_found.recommendation: Change P4 sentence-2 to: 'The dome counts each as a separate confirmed prediction, inflating the WIN total to three entries for what remains a single non-discriminating mechanism — three observational confirmations of Chapman Sq, not three independent dome-derived predictions.' Preserves the count-inflation critique while conceding the legitimate observational independence, which strengthens rather than weakens the attack (the concession sharpens the 'non-discriminating' lever).

holes_found.description: P2 refraction cross-reference, after the c6 hole-3 inoculation, now explicitly states the concern 'does not bear on the Std Model Explains verdict above.' A defender can quote this as admission against interest: 'Your own text concedes the refraction indeterminacy is irrelevant to your verdict — so why is the paragraph in detail_evidence at all? It reads like a gotcha bolted on to a standalone argument.' Hole 3 option (b) — move the paragraph to detail_extra — was not taken; option (a) was. The current text works but option (b) would still be cleaner: relocate the refraction-cascade cross-reference to a 'See also' block in detail_extra so detail_evidence stays on-topic for the primary verdict and the dome-mechanism-incoherence concern lives in a clearly separate channel.
holes_found.recommendation: Low-priority future consideration: move the refraction-cascade paragraph out of detail_evidence into detail_extra or a dedicated 'Related internal contradiction' aside. Current text works; the admission-against-interest phrasing is a minor rhetorical vulnerability, not a blocker.

holes_found.description: Yamazaki & Maute (2021) DOI (10.3389/fspas.2021.766327) remains citations_unchecked from c5 and c6. It is cited in detail_evidence P3 with an active hyperlink but still not externally verified. If the DOI does not resolve, the 'refined by Yamazaki & Maute 2021' construct becomes a dangling reference. Low severity because Chapman 1933 carries the primary citation load, but a defender who pulls the URL and finds 404 would have ammunition.
holes_found.recommendation: Either (a) verify the DOI via a WebFetch in a future cycle, or (b) if unreachable, downgrade the hyperlink to plaintext with a 'citation pending verification' bracket so the uncertainty is inline-visible. The c6 recommendation remains open.

holes_found.description: Chapman 1933 citation ('Terrestrial Magnetism and Atmospheric Electricity 38:3') and Kim & Chang 2018 volume/page (61:2040-2054) remain citations_unchecked. The Chapman 1933 reference format is ambiguous ('38:3' could be Volume 38 issue 3 or Volume 38 page 3); Chapman has multiple 1933 papers, and the canonical foundational Sq citation is often Chapman 1919 (Phil Trans Roy Soc A 218:1-118). The specific 1933 eclipse paper being invoked should be pinned. Kim & Chang 2018 volume/page is plausible for ASR 2018 but unverified against the journal's actual 2018 index.
holes_found.recommendation: Pin Chapman 1933 to a specific paper and verify Kim & Chang 2018 volume/page against ASR's actual 2018 TOC. Low urgency — Chapman's role as foundational Sq theorist is uncontested, but specific citation pinning closes a small credibility gap.

stronger_arguments.description: The WIN-010 / WIN-025 / WIN-068 trio could be reframed as a single count-inflation case study in Part 2b or a kill-shot, rather than three parallel WIN-level critiques. Surfacing the 'three WINs, one mechanism' structure at a higher level would: (i) preempt the defender's observational-independence pushback by showing the critique is about metric inflation not observational double-dipping; (ii) reach readers who do not click through individual WINs; (iii) scale the pattern to other WIN clusters where the same issue may hold (WIN-004/005/006/007 on SAA/NP drift, for example).

The H(r) arithmetic check (see quantitative_verification) showing the FSF 3.2x spread would require ~10,000 km of disc-radius variation deserves explicit inline promotion, not burial in curmudgeon-internal notes. Right now the quantitative impossibility of the FSF spread is implicit in 'no derivation from H(r),' but the explicit arithmetic is a stronger kill than qualitative framing. Proposed inline sentence: 'To produce the observed 3.2x FSF spread purely from H(r) variation, European stations would need to span 10,000 km of disc radius — greater than the pole-to-equator disc distance — whereas they actually span ~4,200 km. Empirical fit is the only explanation left.'
sources: H(r) = 8537 * exp(-r/8619); Kim & Chang 2018 Table 1 station latitudes

kernel_of_truth.description: Kim & Chang (2018) is a genuinely high-quality meta-analysis of 207 geomagnetic manifestations across 39 eclipses — methodologically sound, peer-reviewed, and confirming real patterns (component signs, latitude peak at 30-50°, ~180-min timescale). The dome author's instinct to scale from single-event (WIN-010) to regional (WIN-025) to ensemble (WIN-068) is epistemically reasonable: larger N does improve statistical confidence in the mechanism. The Steelman Evidence Ladder's Tier 1 self-classification shows awareness that the evidence is mechanism-consistent but not mechanism-distinguishing. Credit where due: the literature citation is real, the pattern is real, and the three-level aggregation IS methodologically sound as an accumulation strategy.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The question is discriminatory power, not aggregation quality. Chapman Sq + modern ionospheric modeling predicts Kim & Chang's exact observed pattern from measured/climatological ionospheric inputs with zero free parameters tied to dome geometry. The dome's only quantitative contribution is an FSF scaling factor calibrated directly to the eclipse magnetic signal it purports to predict — a textbook circular fit. Aggregating three successful empirical fits into an ensemble does not convert them into a first-principles derivation. The dome fails the discriminatory-power test at each aggregation level and therefore fails the ensemble test too. The Tier 1 self-classification in the Steelman Ladder is the dome's own quiet concession to this.

deeper_analysis: c6 was a successful patch cycle: the decider self-applied four of the five c6 hole recommendations (holes 1, 2, 3a, 4). Confidence stays essentially flat (0.92 → 0.91) — slight decrease because the hole-2 patch introduced a new 'measured independently by ionosondes and satellite instruments' phrasing that overreaches (see c7 hole 1). The P4 harmonization preserved the self-reference fix but also inherited 'without adding independent evidence,' which a defender can jiu-jitsu — three different events at three aggregation scales ARE independent observations, just not independent predictions. Correct lever is metric inflation, not observational independence (see c7 hole 2). Remaining two moderate holes are both refinements to phrases the patches introduced or preserved; verdict is secure, argument is secure, but two sentences would benefit from sharper phrasing. Residual minors are P2 cosmetics and citation pinning (Yamazaki DOI, Kim & Chang page numbers, Chapman 1933 specific paper). No critical issues. The count-inflation case study at Part 2b / kill-shot level is a strong next-step opportunity that could scale the argument beyond the eclipse trio. Also: c7 advocate_mode now rates defense_survives=3 with a specific defender script citing exactly these three residuals; a c7-to-c8 patch on holes 1 and 2 drops that to 1.

citations_verified: Chapman 1933 as historical Sq reference — role confirmed cited consistently across WIN-010, WIN-025, WIN-068 (exact paper still unpinned)
Kim & Chang 2018 Advances in Space Research 61:2040-2054 — role verified across three sibling WINs; volume/page format plausible but not externally cross-checked this cycle
citations_unchecked: Yamazaki & Maute 2021 Frontiers in Astronomy and Space Sciences doi:10.3389/fspas.2021.766327 — DOI format plausible but not externally resolved; unchanged from c5/c6
Kim & Chang 2018 exact ASR 2018 volume/page index — plausible but not verified against journal TOC

Chapman 1933 Terrestrial Magnetism and Atmospheric Electricity 38:3 — ambiguous citation format; Chapman's canonical foundational Sq paper is often Chapman 1919 Phil Trans Roy Soc A 218:1-118; specific Chapman 1933 paper on eclipse/Sq effects should be pinned
Takeda & Araki 1984 — carried from prior-cycle review narrative; not an active citation in current detail_evidence/detail_verdict text

advocate_mode.best_defense: A careful dome defender, having read our methodological principles, says: 'Your c6 patches are clever but structurally betray their own weakness. P3 now claims Sq models use ionospheric parameters measured independently by ionosondes and satellite instruments — this is empirically overstated. Open Yamazaki & Maute 2021 or any modern Sq paper: ionospheric inputs are climatological composites from IRI, not eclipse-contemporaneous measurements. Your independence claim is as provenance-thin as our FSF calibration — both use empirical tuning of input parameters; the difference is philosophical (fit to output vs fit to other contexts), not categorical. Next, your P4 count-inflation argument implicitly concedes that WIN-010, WIN-025, WIN-068 are three different events at three scales — which is independent evidence by any reasonable definition. You say without adding independent evidence — that is false for the observational base. The metric-inflation critique is legitimate, but you are trying to rhetorically inflate it by denying independence where it clearly exists. Finally, your P2 paragraph explicitly concedes the refraction-cascade point does not bear on the Std Model Explains verdict — so why is it in the primary evidence block? It reads like a gotcha retrofit.' This defense scores 3 — it does not threaten the verdict but would require text changes to fully preempt.
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Apply the three residual hole recommendations: (a) reframe P3 'measured independently' to 'obtained from independent sources, none fitted to the eclipse magnetic signal' — preserves the circularity contrast without overstating ionosonde coverage; (b) reframe P4 'without adding independent evidence' to 'three observational confirmations of one non-discriminating mechanism, not three independent dome-derived predictions' — concedes observational independence while sharpening the metric-inflation lever; (c) long-term, move P2 refraction cross-reference to detail_extra so detail_evidence stays verdict-supporting. Defense drops from 3 to 1 after (a) and (b); (c) is cosmetic. Hole-4 and hole-5 citation pinning closes residual attack surface.

cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: WIN-010, WIN-025, WIN-068 now share aligned Std Model Explains verdicts, aligned Chapman-mechanism framing, and roughly aligned count-inflation phrasing (after c6 patches). P4 harmonization brought WIN-068 into phrase-parity with WIN-025. WIN-010 uses 'three of those claims share one mechanism'; WIN-025 uses 'counting the same eclipse geomagnetic observation three times inflates the WIN count'; WIN-068 now uses 'inflating the score count without adding independent evidence.' All three are phrasing variants of one critique — slight stylistic drift, no semantic inconsistency. Cross-WIN-012 compatibility holds: WIN-012 (1.67 nT/uGal coupling) uses BOU 2017 -10.9 nT as input; our WIN-012 critique does not depend on whether that value is dome-derived or standard-Sq-explained, so it survives the WIN-010 Std-Model-Explains verdict. Recommendation: if the c7 hole-2 rephrasing ('three observational confirmations of one non-discriminating mechanism') is adopted, back-propagate the identical phrasing to WIN-010 and WIN-025 for full triple-harmonization.

claims_checked: FSF range 0.642-2.075 -> 2.075/0.642 = 3.232; text '3.2x spread' correct to 1 d.p. (confirmed, unchanged from c6)
Kim & Chang publication 2018 vs V51.0 early 2026 -> 8-year gap (confirmed, unchanged)

Required disc-radius spread for FSF 3.2x via H alone: delta_r = ln(3.2) * 8619 = 1.163 * 8619 = 10,026 km — arithmetic confirms dome geometry cannot produce observed FSF spread across European station footprint
European station footprint estimate: lat 60N (Lerwick) to 22N (Tamanrasset) ~ 38 deg * 111 km/deg = 4,218 km — order-of-magnitude short of the required 10,026 km
Paragraph count of current detail_evidence = 4 (intro/refraction-xref/Chapman+Sq/Tier-1+count-inflation); structure matches c6 intent

detail_evidence growth 2323 -> 3009 (+686 chars, +30%) aligns with three c6 hole patches: P2 inoculation (~100 chars), P3 Sq-independence clause (~280 chars), P4 harmonization (~300 chars) — sum ~680 chars, consistent with observed growth
detail_verdict growth 1958 -> 1995 (+37 chars, +2%) aligns with hole-4 anchor-link addition (href + onclick ~40 chars)
quantitative_verification.computations: FSF ratio 2.075/0.642 = 3.232 OK. Year gap 2026-2018 = 8 OK. H(r) arithmetic verified: exp(-20000/8619) = 0.09819 -> H(20000) = 838.5 km; exp(-21000/8619) = 0.08745 -> H(21000) = 746.6 km. For FSF 3.2x via H alone: delta_r = ln(3.2) * 8619 = 10,026 km. European station footprint ~4,218 km — insufficient by a factor of 2.4. Count-inflation arithmetic: 3 WINs / 67-WIN total = 4.5% of claimed wins from one non-discriminating mechanism.
quantitative_verification.discrepancies: None. All quantitative claims in current detail_evidence and detail_verdict are internally consistent. The FSF arithmetic remains the strongest quantitative lever and could be promoted to explicit inline computation (stronger_argument #2).
